title: Evaluate piecewise functions
description: Given the formula of a piecewise function, evaluate it for a specific input.

hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $t=\blueD{-10}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{-10}\leq-10$, we should use the first assignment rule $t^2-5t$.

### The answer

$f(\blueD{-10})=(\blueD{-10})^2-5(\blueD{-10})=150$

In conclusion, $f(-10)=150$.

answers.message: You are using a wrong assignment rule!

hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $t=\blueD{17}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{17}=17$, we should use the first assignment rule $\sqrt{17t\,\,}$.

### The answer

$h(\blueD{17})=\sqrt{17 \cdot \blueD{17}\,\,}=17$

In conclusion, $h(17)=17$.

hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $x=\blueD{-18}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{-18}=-18$, we should use the first assignment rule $\dfrac{x-6}{3}$.

### The answer

$f(\blueD{-18})=\dfrac{\blueD{-18}-6}{3}=-8$

In conclusion, $f(-18)=-8$.

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $t=\blueD{12}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $6\leq \blueD{12}\leq12$, we should use the second assignment rule $\dfrac{5t}{6}$.

### The answer

$g(\blueD{12})=\dfrac{5\cdot\blueD{12}}{6}=10$

In conclusion, $g(12)=10$.

hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $x=\blueD{-5}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{-5}\geq -5$, we should use the third assignment rule $4x-3$.

### The answer

$h(\blueD{-5})=4\cdot(\blueD{-5})-3=-23$

In conclusion, $h(-5)=-23$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $3\leq \blueD{4}\leq 10$, we should use the second assignment rule $x^2-7$.

### The answer

$f(\blueD{4})=\blueD{4}^2-7=9$

In conclusion, $f(4)=9$.

hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $x=\blueD{1}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{1}\neq-1$ and $\blueD{1} \neq -2$, we should use the third assignment rule $ x^3-x^2+1$.

### The answer

$g(\blueD{1})= \blueD{1}^3-\blueD{1}^2+1=1$

In conclusion, $g(1)=1$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{6}\neq0$ and $\blueD{6} \neq 7$, we should use the third assignment rule $3x-2$.

In conclusion, $h(6)=16$.

### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $t=\blueD{2}$.

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{2}\neq8$ and $\blueD{2} \neq 10$, we should use the third assignment rule $ t^2-3t+2$.

### The answer

$f(\blueD{2})= \blueD{2}^2-3\cdot\blueD{2}+2=0$

In conclusion, $f(2)=0$.

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $x=\blueD{-3}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{-3}\in(-\infty,0]$, we should use the first assignment rule $x^3$.

### The answer

$h(\blueD{-3})=(\blueD{-3})^3=-27$

In conclusion, $h(-3)=-27$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$h(t) = \begin{cases}
\dfrac{t}{3}+6& , &  t\in (-\infty,-3) \\\\
t(t+4) & , & t \in [-3,3)\\\\
t^2+t+1 & , & t \in [3,\infty)\end{cases}$

$h(0)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $t=\blueD{0}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{0}\in[-3,3)$, we should use the second assignment rule $t(t+4)$.

### The answer

$h(\blueD{0})=\blueD{0}(\blueD{0}+4)=0$

In conclusion, $h(0)=0$.

hints:
### The strategy

First, we should find the appropriate assignment rule out of the three, by checking which case applies for $x=\blueD{7}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

### Finding the appropriate assignment rule

Since $\blueD{7}\in(2,\infty)$, we should use the third assignment rule $(x+1)(x-5)$.

### The answer

$g(\blueD{7})=(\blueD{7}+1)(\blueD{7}-5)=16$

In conclusion, $g(7)=16$.
